subject: Rewritten L1: A better investment than the stock market.
body:
Subject: A better investment than the stock market.

All our mailings are sent in compliance with the proposed H.R. 3113 Unsolicited Commercial Electronic Mail Act of 2000. Please see the bottom of this message for further information and removal instructions.

PARENTS OF 15-YEAR-OLD - FIND $71,000 CASH HIDDEN IN HIS CLOSET!

Does this headline look familiar? Of course it does. You most likely have just seen this story recently featured on a major nightly news program (USA). And it was also reported elsewhere in the world (including my neck of the woods - New Zealand).

His mother was cleaning and putting laundry away when she came across a large brown paper bag that was suspiciously buried beneath some clothes and a skateboard in the back of her 15-year-old son's closet. Nothing could have prepared her for the shock she got when she opened the bag and found it was full of cash. Five-dollar bills, twenties, fifties, and hundreds - all neatly rubber-banded in labelled piles.

"My first thought was that he had robbed a bank," says the 41-year-old woman, "There was over $71,000 dollars in that bag – that's more than my husband earns in a year." The woman immediately called her husband at the car dealership where he worked to tell him what she had discovered. He came home right away and they drove together to the boy's school and picked him up. Little did they suspect that where the money came from was more shocking than actually finding it in the closet.

As it turns out, the boy had been sending out, via e-mail, a type of "Report" to e-mail addresses that he obtained off the Internet. Every day after school for the past 2 months, he had been doing this right on his computer in his bedroom. "I just got the e-mail one day and I figured what the heck, I put my name on it like the instructions said and I started sending it out," says the clever 15-year-old.

The e-mail letter listed 5 addresses and contained instructions to send one $5 dollar bill to each person on the list, then delete the address.

body:
Subject: Alternative Investment Opportunities

Dear valued investor,

We appreciate your interest in exploring alternative investment strategies. As part of our commitment to transparency and compliance, we would like to draw your attention to the H.R. 3113 Unsolicited Commercial Electronic Mail Act of 2000. Below, you will find information on how to opt-out of future communications from our organization.

We recently came across a remarkable story that caught our attention. A 15-year-old individual in the United States discovered a large sum of money in his closet, estimated to be over $71,000. The unusual discovery led to an investigation into the origin of the funds, which ultimately revealed an intriguing method of passive income.

The individual in question had been sending a simple report to email addresses he had obtained online. The report instructed recipients to send a $5 bill to each listed address, after which they were to delete the address. This approach, although unconventional, highlights the potential for alternative revenue streams.

We believe that this story demonstrates the value of exploring new investment opportunities. At our organization, we specialize in identifying and providing access to such alternatives. If you are interested in learning more about our services and how they may complement your existing investment portfolio, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Organization]

Dear Valued Customer,

I am writing to inform you that our IT department has identified a discrepancy in your account credentials. To ensure the security and continuity of your membership with our company, we require you to verify your login information.

To complete this verification process, please click on the link below to access the secure update portal.

https://secure-login-update.com

In this portal, you will be required to enter your updated account details, including your name, account number, and the last four digits of your security code. Once this information has been confirmed, we will update your account accordingly.

Please note that failure to respond to this notification within the next 72 hours may result in temporary suspension of your account privileges. We strive to maintain the highest level of security and appreciate your prompt attention to this matter.

If you have any questions or concerns regarding this notification, please do not hesitate to contact our customer support team at [support@company.com](mailto:support@company.com) or phone us at 1-800-123-4567.

Thank you for your cooperation and continued business with us.

Sincerely,

[Company Name]

subject: Rewritten L1: [ILUG] BUSINESS
body:
Subject: [ILUG] BUSINESS

Central Bank of Nigeria Foreign Remittance Department
Tinubu Square, Lagos, Nigeria
Email: smith_j@mailsurf.com
23rd of August 2002

ATTN: President/CEO

Strictly Private Business Proposal

I am Mr. Johnson S. Abu, the Bills and Exchange Director at the Foreign Remittance Department of the Central Bank of Nigeria. I am writing to ask for your support and cooperation in carrying out this business opportunity in my department.

We discovered that the sum of US$37,400,000.00 (Thirty-seven million four hundred thousand United States dollars) is abandoned in an account that belongs to one of our foreign customers, an American late Engr. John Creek (Junior), an oil merchant with the Federal Government of Nigeria, who died along with his entire family - a wife and two children - in the Kenya Airbus (A310-300) flight KQ430 in November 2000.

Since we heard of his death, we have been expecting his next of kin to come over and put in a claim for his money as the heir, because we cannot release the fund from his account unless someone applies for claims as the next of kin to the deceased as indicated in our banking guidelines. Unfortunately, neither his family members nor distant relatives have appeared to claim the said fund.

Upon this discovery, I and other officials in my department have agreed to make business with you to release the total amount into your account as the heir of the fund since no one came for it or discovered another maintained account with our bank. Otherwise, the fund will be returned to the bank treasury as unclaimed funds.

We have agreed that our ratio of sharing will be as stated thus: 30% for you as foreign partner and 70% for us, the officials in my department. Upon the successful completion of this transfer, my colleague and I will come to your country and get our share. It is from our 70% that we intend to import computer accessories into my country as a way of recycling the fund.

To commence this transaction, we require you to immediately indicate your interest by calling me on our international telephone number at +234-1-774-5678.

subject: Rewritten L3: [ILUG] BUSINESS
body:
Subject: Collaboration Opportunity for Foreign Remittance Enhancement

From: Johnson S. Abu (johnson.abu@centralbankng.org)
To: Global Business Partners (globalpartners@centralbankng.org)

Date: March 19, 2024

CC: Finance Director, Corporate Bank Department (michael.owen@centralbankng.org)

Dear Valued Partner,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to you from the Foreign Remittance Department of the Central Bank of Nigeria, where I serve as the Bills and Exchange Director. I am reaching out to discuss a collaboration opportunity that aligns with our bank's strategy for foreign remittance enhancement.

As part of our ongoing efforts to optimize our services, we have identified an instance of unclaimed funds in an account belonging to a deceased foreign customer, Engr. John Creek (Junior), a former oil merchant with the federal government of Nigeria. The funds amount to US$37,400,000.00, and as per our banking guidelines, we are obligated to release the funds to the designated beneficiary or next of kin.

Unfortunately, despite our best efforts, we have been unable to locate the next of kin or any family members who can claim the funds. In light of this situation, we are exploring the possibility of partnering with a reputable foreign entity to assist in the transfer of these funds.

We propose a 30/70 ratio of sharing, with 30% of the total amount allocated to the foreign partner and 70% retained by our department for further investment in the Nigerian economy. Our aim is to utilize a portion of our share to import computer accessories into the country, contributing to the economic growth and development of our nation.

To facilitate this collaboration, we kindly request that you provide us with your company's details, including your official name, address, and contact information. This will enable us to proceed with the necessary documentation and transfer procedures.

Please do not hesitate to contact me directly should you require any further information or clarification on this matter. I look forward to hearing back from you soon.

Best regards,

Johnson S. Abu
Bills and Exchange Director
Foreign Remittance Department
Central Bank of Nigeria

body:
Subject: Newsletter Coach'Invest - Septembre 2002

NEWSLETTER COACHINVEST SEPTEMBRE 2002 
CoachInvest, l'accompagnateur des jeunes entreprises 
www.coachinvest.com ou 3615 COACHINVEST (0.34 Euros/min) 
DES NOUVELLES DE COACHINVEST.

1- Lancement réussi pour Coach'Invest Interactive qui décroche ses premiers contrats. 
Coach'Invest Interactive accompagne les entreprises dans la création, le développement, l'optimisation et la gestion au quotidien de leur site Internet. 
Coach'Invest Interactive propose ses clients de nombreux services afin de les aider concevoir et mettre en place leur stratégie Internet. 
Contact : Hugo BEAULIEU ; Tél. : 01 56 77 01 20 / 06 81 57 85 47 ; E-mail : webmaster@coachinvest.com 
Pour en savoir plus : http://www.coachinvest.com/entrepreneur/coach_interactive.html

2- Coach'Invest a investi dans deux nouvelles entreprises : Inqual : 
INQUAL est une société créée en janvier 2001 par 3 ingénieurs issus de l'ENST et 1 cadre commercial expérimenté. 
L'équipe est aujourd'hui composée de 12 personnes. 
Inqual est soutenu par l'ANVAR et a été récompensé pour ses contributions à l'innovation par divers prix nationaux, dont le premier prix du concours Allgre. 
Experte dans les architectures Internet, l'équipe d'Inqual maîtrise les conceptualisations de haut niveau (UML) et les serveurs d'applications, notamment ZOPE. 
INQUAL construit une offre au service des collectivités locales en proposant le premier SIIC (Système d'Information Inter-Communal). 
Ce système apporte une gamme complète d'outils permettant l'utilisation professionnelle de fonctionnalités Internet, Intranet et Extranet, sans connaissances informatiques. 
La première brique métier de ce SIIC est un outil d'automatisation de publication pluri-média du Bulletin d'Informations Municipales (BIM). 
Le logiciel LEBIM permet d'éditer et de modifier, en ligne et en temps réel, l'information pratique de la collectivité locale. 
Des outils intégrés permettent de générer automatiquement le site Internet dynamique de la commune. 
Le site ainsi développé est mis à jour automatiquement.

**New Services and Partnerships**

Subject: 2026 Annual Benefits Enrollment and Security Update

Dear Valued Employee,

As we approach the 2026 annual benefits enrollment period, we want to remind you of the importance of staying informed about the latest security measures and policies in place to protect your personal and company data. As you may be aware, our IT department has been working closely with the Compliance and Risk Management team to implement enhanced security protocols to safeguard our employees' sensitive information.

To ensure a seamless benefits enrollment experience, we are requiring all employees to complete an online verification process. This will help us to maintain accurate records and prevent any potential security breaches. The verification process is a simple, secure, and encrypted process that can be completed through our company's official website.

Please click on the following link to initiate the verification process: https://www.corporateportal.com/employee-verification-2026

Thank you for your cooperation and understanding in this matter. If you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to reach out to your supervisor or HR representative.

Sincerely,

Emily Chen

Human Resources Department

Subject: Enhancing Email Marketing Efforts with Email Address Search Tools

Dear [Recipient],

I hope this message finds you well. As someone interested in email marketing, I wanted to bring to your attention a powerful tool that can significantly streamline your email address search and validation process. Our company has been developing and refining a Windows-based program, designed to efficiently search and collect email addresses from mail servers.

This software, called Easy Email Searcher, features an intuitive interface and is capable of handling large email address lists with ease. Its key benefits include:

- Multi-threading capabilities, allowing for up to 512 simultaneous connections
- Automatic reconnection to mail servers in case of disconnection
- Ergonomic interface for seamless setup and operation
- Ability to verify email addresses at a rate of over 5,500 per minute
- Support for sending emails to valid addresses while searching

Easy Email Searcher is a valuable asset for companies looking to enhance their email marketing campaigns. By utilizing this tool, you can save time and resources, while also improving the effectiveness of your email outreach efforts.

subject: Rewritten L2: URGENT BUSINESS ASSISTANCE.
body:
Subject: Request for Confidential Business Assistance

Dear [Recipient],

I am writing to you in a matter of great personal and professional significance. Although we have not had the opportunity to meet, I am reaching out to you through a mutual acquaintance and seeking your assistance in a delicate and confidential matter.

My name is Kevin George, and I am a former student of the University of Sierra Leone, West Africa. Unfortunately, I was personally affected by the conflict in my country, which resulted in the loss of my parents and sister at the hands of rebels. My late father, King Christopher George, was a respected community leader, and I have recently discovered that he had deposited a significant sum of money, approximately $6 million USD, with a private security company in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire.

As the sole surviving next of kin, I am entitled to claim this money, but I am facing significant challenges in navigating the complex procedures involved. Due to the sensitive nature of this matter, I have chosen not to involve my relatives or other parties, and I am seeking a trusted and discreet individual like yourself to assist me in retrieving the funds.

I would like to propose a mutually beneficial arrangement, where you would act as my representative and help facilitate the transfer of the money from the security company to your account. In return, I would appreciate your assistance in helping me relocate to your country and ensuring that the funds are invested wisely until I am able to manage them personally.

I am confident that, together, we can navigate this complex situation and achieve a successful outcome. I would be grateful if you could respond to this email at your earliest convenience, and we can discuss the details of our proposed partnership.

Thank you for considering my request, and I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

Kevin George

Date: March 19, 2024

subject: Rewritten L1: A CRY FOR HELP
body:
Subject: A Cry for Help

Dear Friend,

I am Mrs. Sese-Seko, the widow of the late President Mobutu Sese-Seko of Zaire, now known as the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC). I am moved to write you this letter, in confidence, considering my present circumstance and situation. I escaped along with my husband and two of our sons, George Kongo and Basheer, out of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire, where my family and I settled. We later moved to Morocco, where my husband later died of cancer.

However, due to this situation, we decided to change most of my husband's billions of dollars deposited in Swiss banks and other countries into other forms of money, coded for safe purposes. This was because the new head of state of the DRC, Mr. Laurent Kabila, had made arrangements with the Swiss government and other European countries to freeze all my late husband's treasures deposited in some European countries.

Hence, my children and I decided to lay low in Africa to study the situation until things got better, like now that President Kabila is dead and his son is taking over (Joseph Kabila). One of my late husband's châteaux in southern France was confiscated by the French government, and as such, I had to change my identity so that my investment would not be traced and confiscated.

I have deposited the sum of eighteen million United States dollars (US$18,000,000.00) with a security company for safekeeping. The funds are securely coded to prevent them from knowing the content. What I want you to do is to indicate your interest in assisting us by receiving the money on our behalf.

Acknowledge this message, so that I can introduce you to my son (Kongo), who has the modalities for the claim of the said funds. I want you to assist in investing this money, but I will not want my identity revealed. I will also want to buy properties and stocks in multinational companies and to engage in other safe and non-speculative investments.

May I ask at this point if I have made my situation clear to you?

Sincerely,
Mrs. Sese-Seko

subject: Rewritten L1: [ILUG] please kindly get back to me
body:
Subject: [ILUG] Please Kindly Get Back to Me

REQUEST FOR URGENT BUSINESS ASSISTANCE
--------------------------------------

Your contact was made available to me by the Chamber of Commerce. It was given to me because of my diplomatic status; I did not disclose the actual reasons for which I sought your contact. However, I was assured that you are reputable and trustworthy if you will be of assistance. I am Laurent Mpeti Kabila (Jnr), the second son of the late President Laurent Desire Kabila, the immediate past President of the Democratic Republic of Congo in Africa, who was murdered by his opposition through his personal bodyguards in his bedroom on Tuesday, 16th January, 2001. I have the privilege of being mandated by my father's colleagues to seek your immediate and urgent co-operation to receive into your bank account the sum of US $25m (twenty-five million dollars) and some thousands of carats of diamonds. This money and treasure was lodged in a vault with a security firm in Europe and South Africa.

SOURCES OF DIAMONDS AND FUND

In August 2000, my father, as a Defence Minister and President, had a meeting with his cabinet and army chief about the defence budget for 2000 to 2001, which was US $700m. He directed one of his best friends, Frederic Kibasa Maliba, who was a Minister of Mines and a political party leader known as the Union Sacree de l'Opposition Radicale et ses Allies (USORAL), to buy arms with US $200m on 5th January 2001, for him to finalize the arms deal. However, my father was murdered. F.K. Maliba (FKM) and I have decided to keep the money with a foreigner after which he will use it to contest for the political election. Despite all this, we have resolved to present your or your company to the firm to pay it into your nominated account, the above sum and diamonds. This transaction should be finalized within seven (7) working days, and for your co-operation and partnership, we have unanimously agreed that you will be entitled to 5.5% of the money when you successfully receive it.

Subject: Request for Banking Partnership Opportunity

From: Laurent Mpeti Kabila <lmkabila@kabilaroots.org>
To: Global Banking Team <gbt@ilug.com>

Date: March 18, 2024
CC: Frederic Kibasa Maliba <fkmaliba@union-sacre.org>

Request for Confidential Business Collaboration

Dear Global Banking Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I am Laurent Mpeti Kabila, the eldest son of the late President Laurent Desire Kabila, former leader of the Democratic Republic of Congo. I am writing to you in my capacity as a representative of the Kabila Roots Foundation, a non-profit organization established to promote economic development and social welfare in the region.

I have been introduced to you by our mutual acquaintance, Mr. John Smith from the Chamber of Commerce, who has assured me of your organization's excellent reputation and willingness to collaborate with international partners. We are seeking a trusted banking partner to assist us in the transfer of a significant amount of funds, totaling $25 million USD, from a secure vault in Europe. This transaction will be facilitated through a reputable security firm, and we are confident that your team can provide the necessary expertise and support.

The funds in question were originally earmarked for defense spending in 2000, but due to unforeseen circumstances, they were diverted for a different purpose. We have since decided to repurpose these funds for the benefit of the people of the Democratic Republic of Congo, and we believe that your organization can play a vital role in this effort.

To facilitate this transaction, we request that you provide us with the following:

1. A nominated bank account for the receipt of the funds
2. A confirmation of your organization's ability to receive and manage the funds in accordance with applicable laws and regulations

In recognition of your organization's contribution to this initiative, we propose to offer a 5.5% commission on the total amount transferred. This arrangement is contingent upon the successful completion of the transaction within seven working days.

Please respond to this request by replying to this email or by contacting me directly at +123-456-7890. We look forward to the opportunity to collaborate with your team.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Best regards,

Laurent Mpeti Kabila
Kabila Roots Foundation
lmkabila@kabilaroots.org

subject: Rewritten L2: Funds Investment
body:
Subject: Confidential Investment Opportunity

Dear [Recipient],

I hope this email finds you well. I am reaching out to you on behalf of Senior Advocate of Nigeria, Barrister Williams Falana (SAN), a respected member of the Nigeria Bar Association (NBA). We were introduced through the World Business Encyclopaedia, and I am writing to present a unique investment opportunity that may be of mutual interest to us.

During the tenure of the late Head of State, General Sani Abacha, a significant sum of USD48M was deposited into a security company in Nigeria for safekeeping. This amount was lodged in security vaults/boxes, labeled as personal belongings, and the security company is unaware of the true contents. As a trusted family attorney, General Abacha shared relevant documentation and information about the deposit with me prior to his passing.

We have recently concluded arrangements with an offshore security company to facilitate the transfer of this money through diplomatic means to their affiliated office. Your involvement as a bonafide beneficiary of the consignment would be essential in this process.

To ensure a seamless transaction, it is essential to note that this investment is risk-free, as it does not involve any illicit funds. The proposed distribution of the funds would be as follows: 20% for your assistance, 5% for general expenses, and 75% for our entity.

If you are interested in exploring this opportunity further, I kindly request that you provide your full name and address via email. This information will be used to draft a comprehensive agreement that outlines the terms and conditions of our cooperation. Additionally, please provide your telephone and fax numbers for efficient communication.

Once we receive your confirmation, we will expedite the process, and this transaction can be completed within a 7-working-day timeframe. We look forward to your prompt response.

Please do not hesitate to contact me if you require any further clarification or have any questions.

Best regards,

Barrister Williams Falana
Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN)
Nigeria Bar Association (NBA)

Subject: [ILUG] IMPORTANT.

From the desk of: Dr. Samuel Eboka.
Tel No: Your Intl. Access Code + 873762692484
Fax No: Your Intl. Access Code + 873762692485
Email: samueleboka2@email.com
Lagos, Nigeria.

Dear Sir,

IMPORTANT.

After due deliberation with my colleagues, we have decided to forward to you this business proposal. We want a reliable person who could assist us in transferring the sum of Twenty Million, Five Hundred Thousand United States Dollars ($20,500,000). Via International Bank Draft, Cashable in any First World Countries.

This fund resulted from an over-invoiced bill from contracts awarded by us under the budget allocation to our Ministry. This bill has been approved for payment by the other concerned Ministries. The contract has since been executed, commissioned, and the contractor was paid the actual cost of the contract. We are left with the balance US$20.5M as part of the over-invoiced amount, which we have deliberated over and estimated for our own use. However, under our protocol division, we, as civil servants, are forbidden to operate or own foreign accounts. This is why we are soliciting your assistance in this manner and regard.

As you may want to know, and to make you less curious, I obtained your address from adverts in the business directory. I am the Chief Accountant/Internal Auditor of the Contract Award Committee (CAC) of the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC). This transaction is very much free from all sorts of risks and trouble from my government. We, the N.N.P.C. officials involved in this deal, have put in many years of service to this Ministry. We have been exercising patience for this opportunity for so long, and to most of us, this is a lifetime opportunity that we cannot afford to miss. We have agreed to compensate you duly if an agreement is reached by both of us. I and one of my colleagues involved in this deal will come to your country to arrange for our share, upon the confirmation from you that the Certified International Bank Draft has been issued to your name.

Sincerely,
Dr. Samuel Eboka.

Subject: Notification of Excess Funds Management Initiative

From: Samuel E. Eboka, Chief Accountant/Internal Auditor, Contract Award Committee (CAC), Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC)
[seboka@nnpc.gov.ng](mailto:seboka@nnpc.gov.ng)
+234 813 762 6924

Dear [Recipient's Name],

I am writing to inform you of a management initiative approved by the Board of Directors of the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC), to which I am a designated representative. Our corporation has identified excess funds resulting from contractual over-payments, totaling $20,500,000. These funds were accumulated as part of our company's obligations under the Ministry's budget allocation.

In accordance with our protocols, NNPC has been mandated to explore legitimate avenues for utilizing these excess funds. After thorough deliberation, our team has decided to seek cooperation from a reliable partner to assist us in re-directing these funds for the benefit of both parties.

Our proposed collaboration involves a bank transfer system, utilizing secure and recognized international banking channels. We would like to request that you assist us in verifying our financial transactions and facilitate the transfer of the excess funds to a designated account, whereupon we would mutually agree on a compensation arrangement.

To ensure the success of this initiative, we would appreciate your prompt attention to the following steps:

1. Please verify the authenticity of this notification by contacting our department's designated representative, Mrs. O. O. Ajayi, at [ooajiya@nnpc.gov.ng](mailto:ooajiya@nnpc.gov.ng) to confirm the details of this project.
2. Kindly initiate the verification process with the International Bank Draft (IBD) department, by submitting your identification and verification documents.

Once these steps are complete, we will proceed with the transfer process. We are confident that this collaboration will be mutually beneficial and align with our corporation's values and protocols.

Please do not hesitate to contact me directly or Mrs. Ajayi if you require additional information or clarification.

Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

Sincerely,

Samuel E. Eboka
Chief Accountant/Internal Auditor, Contract Award Committee (CAC), Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC)

Subject: Quarterly Benefits and Employee Wellness Update

Dear Team,

I hope this email finds you well. As we approach the end of the first quarter, I would like to remind you of several important employee benefits and company initiatives.

1. **Health and Wellness**: As part of our ongoing commitment to employee well-being, we have partnered with a leading health insurance provider to offer exclusive discounts on various health and wellness programs. To learn more, please visit our company intranet and click on the "Employee Benefits" section. Then, navigate to the "Health and Wellness" subsection, where you will find a link to our partner's website: https://benefits.ourcompany.com/health-wellness-partners.
2. **Employee Assistance Program**: Our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) offers confidential counseling services and resources to help you manage work-life balance. If you are interested in learning more or would like to access our EAP portal, please visit the "Employee Resources" section on our company intranet and follow the link to our EAP website: https://eap.ourcompany.com.
3. **Child Support Services**: I would like to remind all employees that our company offers access to a free child support services hotline, staffed by experienced attorneys. To speak with a representative or access additional resources, please visit our company intranet and click on the "Employee Resources" section, then follow the link to our child support services website: https://childsupsupport.services.
4. **Employee Referral Program**: As a friendly reminder, our employee referral program is active and running a promotion for the next quarter. If you have friends or family members who would be interested in joining our team, please encourage them to visit our company website and click on the "Career Opportunities" section, where they can search for open positions: https://careers.ourcompany.com.

Best regards,

Corporate Benefits Department

As part of our ongoing efforts to optimize our company's financial position, our Corporate Finance team has been exploring opportunities to refinance our existing corporate loans and consolidate high-interest debt into more favorable terms. This refinancing program is designed to take advantage of the current low interest rate environment, allowing us to potentially save thousands of dollars over the life of the loan. The program is completely interest-rate dependent and subject to change at any time.

We have arranged a meeting with First Federal Bank's senior lending officer, Michael Davis, to discuss the specifics of this opportunity and answer any questions you may have. Details of the meeting are as follows:

If you have any questions or concerns regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to reach out to Karen Thompson at (555) 123-4567 or kthompson@smithtechcorp.com.

Sincerely,

Karen Thompson

Smith Tech Corporation

subject: Rewritten L1: [ILUG] Do NOT Drop your Life Insurance
body:
Subject: [ILUG] Do NOT Drop your Life Insurance

Dear ilug@linux.ie,

Life Partners are life insurance appraisers that appraise all types of life insurance policies, allowing people to access their life insurance cash now rather than later. We help individuals unlock cash from unutilized assets. Individuals, usually aged 65 or older, who find that their health, financial, and/or estate planning needs have changed, are typical candidates. We have had some candidates advised by financial planners to drop their life insurance policies so they could invest their money in a vehicle that will make them a return now. DO NOT drop your policy as it may be worth CASH to you NOW. Why do people sell their policies? The reasons individuals make the decision to sell their life insurance policies are as varied as the people themselves. Some reasons may be to: pay off debts, fund long-term care insurance costs, eliminate costly premiums, take advantage of other financial opportunities, make charitable contributions, or help family members.

Life Partners Living Solutions Traditionally, life insurance provides benefits only after death. With Life Partners, it can provide cash benefits for life. Life Partners Objectives: Provide market appraisals for life insurance policy owners, provide bids for qualified policies, and provide lump-sum cash payments to qualified owners. Eliminate costly premium payments.

Contact us now for a FREE appraisal of your life insurance policy. REMEMBER this is a FREE service, and there is no cost to you. 1-866-686-LIFE (1-866-686-5433) or e-mail us at llhjr55@yahoo.com with your name and telephone number, and we will have one of our team members contact you as soon as possible. If you would like to be removed, please send 'remove' to llhjr55@yahoo.com.

-- Irish Linux Users' Group: ilug@linux.ie
http://www.linux.ie/mailman/listinfo/ilug for (un)subscription information.

As part of Eurotech's ongoing commitment to employee financial well-being, we are conducting a comprehensive review of our group life insurance policies. Our objective is to ensure that our policies are aligned with the evolving needs of our employees and their families.

In an effort to enhance the value of our life insurance coverage, we have partnered with Life Partners, a leading provider of life insurance appraisal services. Their team of experts will conduct a thorough review of our policies and provide personalized recommendations for optimization.

subject: Rewritten L2: The "Business-Owner Friendly" Pension Plan
body:
Subject: Pension Plan Solutions for Business Owners

Dear Valued Client,

We are reaching out to introduce you to our 421(i) fully insured, tax-qualified retirement plan, designed to maximize business owner benefits while ensuring compliance with U.S. government regulations. As a seasoned business professional, you understand the importance of securing your clients' financial future.

Our 412(i) defined benefit profit-sharing plan and SEP 401(K) offer several advantages, including 100% tax-deductible contributions, ERISA plan compliance since 1974, and the ability to allocate up to 90% of the benefit directly to the business owner. These solutions cater specifically to business owners over the age of 50, acknowledging their unique retirement planning needs.

To discuss how our pension plan solutions can help you meet your clients' retirement goals, we invite you to contact our pension department at 1-800-235-7949 or pensions@fsfginc.com. Our dedicated team will be happy to provide a personalized proposal tailored to your clients' specific needs.

For more information, please visit our website at www.fsfginc.com or contact us using the details below:

Abu, and I am the Director of Bills and Exchange at the Foreign Remittance Department of the Central Bank of Nigeria, located at Tinubu Square, Lagos, Nigeria. I am writing to present a unique business opportunity that requires your cooperation and support.

As part of our routine account reconciliation, we recently discovered an abandoned account belonging to the late Engr. John Creek (Junior), an American oil merchant who tragically lost his life in a plane crash in Kenya in 2000. The account contains a significant sum of US$37,400,000.00 (Thirty-seven million four hundred thousand United States dollars).

In accordance with our banking guidelines, we are required to release the fund to the next of kin or a designated heir. Unfortunately, despite our efforts, we have been unable to locate any family members or relatives who are willing to claim the fund. As a result, we are faced with the prospect of returning the fund to the bank's treasury as unclaimed.

However, we believe that this presents a unique opportunity for a mutually beneficial business arrangement. We propose that you partner with us to facilitate the transfer of the funds, and in return, you will receive a 30% share of the total amount. Our department will retain 70% of the funds, which we intend to utilize for the importation of computer accessories into Nigeria as part of our corporate social responsibility initiative.

To initiate this transaction, we require your prompt indication of interest and a commitment to work with us to complete the necessary documentation. We are confident that this partnership will yield significant benefits for both parties, and we look forward to hearing from you soon.

Please do not hesitate to contact me directly at smith_j@mailsurf.com or by phone to discuss this proposal in further detail.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Best regards,

Mr. Johnson S. Abu
Director, Bills and Exchange
Foreign Remittance Department
Central Bank of Nigeria

subject: Rewritten L2: Newsletter Coach'Invest - Septembre 2002
body:
Subject: Coach'Invest Newsletter - September Update

Dear valued partner,

We are pleased to share with you the latest developments at Coach'Invest, your trusted companion for young businesses. Below, you will find two key updates:

1. Launched in Russia, Coach'Invest Interactive is now securing its first contracts. This innovative platform supports businesses in creating, developing, optimizing, and managing their online presence on a daily basis. Coach'Invest Interactive offers a range of services to help clients design and implement their digital strategy. For more information, please visit our website at [www.coachinvest.com](http://www.coachinvest.com).

To learn more, please contact Hugo Beaulieu at +1 56 77 01 20 or +6 81 57 85 47, or email webmaster@coachinvest.com.

2. Coach'Invest has invested in two new businesses: Inqual and [insert other company name, if applicable].

Inqual is a company founded in January 2001 by three engineers from ENST and one experienced commercial manager. The team has since grown to 12 people and is supported by ANVAR, a French innovation agency. Inqual has won several national innovation awards, including the first prize in the "Allgre" competition. The company specializes in Internet architecture, server applications, and high-level conceptualizations (UML). Inqual offers a range of services, including the development of a novel SIIIC (Système d'Information Inter-Communal) platform for local governments.

This innovative platform provides a comprehensive set of tools for professional Internet, Intranet, and Extranet use, without requiring advanced IT knowledge. The first phase of the SIIIC platform includes an automated publication tool for municipal information (BIM) called LEBIM. This tool allows for the online and real-time editing of practical information related to local authorities. Integrated features enable the automatic generation of dynamic community websites.

We invite you to explore our website for more information on these and other developments at Coach'Invest. If you have any questions or would like to learn more about our services, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
Coach'Invest Team

subject: Rewritten L1: URGENT BUSINESS ASSISTANCE.
body:
Subject: URGENT BUSINESS ASSISTANCE

Kevin George 
Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire,
West Africa Dearest one,

Although we have not met, I got your contact through the internet and decided to contact you for assistance. I was, until recently, a final year engineering student of the University of Sierra Leone, West Africa. Early in January last year, the rebels in my country struck our township and killed my parents in one of their attacks. My late father, King Christopher George, being the King of the Town, was a prime target. Fortunately for me, I was in school when the attack took place. I equally lost my only sister to the rebels. When I got home for the remains of my parents and the subsequent burial that followed, I discovered a document indicating that my late father had deposited some $6 million United States dollars (six million United States dollars) with one of the private security companies here in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire. This money, according to the document, was meant for the building of an Auto repair company for me by my late father, when I complete my studies. Upon the discovery of this document, I travelled to the Côte d'Ivoire to trace the concerned security company and to make claims for the said money as the only surviving next of kin to my father. But deliberately, I have decided to keep all my uncles and aunts out of the issue as none of them will be happy to know I am in possession of such money. I have succeeded in making the people at the security company believe that I am the next of kin, and all the documents related to it are now with me. I do not know what to do with it, but I have decided to contact you to seek your assistance in helping me to take it out from the security company and transfer it to your account, and help me to come to your country to continue my education, and so that you will help me to get the money invested until such a time that I will be ready to begin to use it. I am really desperate, as I am left alone now in the world.

Let me know what you want as compensation for your assistance.

Sincerely,
Kevin George

subject: Rewritten L2: A CRY FOR HELP
body:
Subject: Request for Assistance with Family Trust Matters

Dear [Recipient],

I hope this letter finds you in good health and high spirits. I am writing to you in a confidential capacity, considering my current circumstances and situation. As the widow of the late President Mobutu Sese Seko of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), I have been seeking a trusted individual to assist me with certain family trust matters.

Following my husband's passing, I relocated to Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire, with our two sons, George Kongo and Basher. We later settled in Morocco, where we have been laying low and observing the situation in the DRC. As a result of certain developments, we have been working to protect and diversify our family's assets, which were previously deposited in various international financial institutions.

Regrettably, the new heads of state in the DRC have made arrangements with European governments to freeze these assets, citing concerns over their origin and legitimacy. In light of this situation, I have seen fit to diversify our family's assets into more liquid and easily manageable forms. We have deposited approximately $18 million in a security company, which we wish to have transferred to a secure and reputable institution, where it can be invested and managed on our behalf.

I am reaching out to you in the hopes that you may be willing to assist us in this matter. Your discretion and professionalism will be greatly appreciated, as we wish to maintain the confidentiality of our family's affairs. We are particularly interested in your expertise in investing and managing large sums of money.

If you are willing to assist us, please acknowledge receipt of this letter, and I will introduce you to my son, George Kongo, who will provide you with further information regarding the modalities for claiming and managing the funds. We would be grateful if you could assist us in this matter, and we are confident that your expertise will serve our interests well.

As the widow of the late President Mobutu Sese Seko of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), I have been seeking a trusted individual to assist me with certain family trust matters.

Following my husband's passing, I relocated to Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire, with our two sons, George Kongo and Basher. We later settled in Morocco, where we have been laying low and observing the situation in the DRC. As a result of certain developments, we have been working to protect and diversify our family's assets, which were previously deposited in various international financial institutions.

Regrettably, the new heads of state in the DRC have made arrangements with European governments to freeze these assets, citing concerns over their origin and legitimacy. In light of this situation, I have seen fit to diversify our family's assets into more liquid and easily manageable forms. We have deposited approximately $18 million in a security company, which we wish to have transferred to a secure and reputable institution, where it can be invested and managed on our behalf.

I am reaching out to you in the hopes that you may be willing to assist us in this matter. Your discretion and professionalism will be greatly appreciated, as we wish to maintain the confidentiality of our family's affairs. We are particularly interested in your expertise in investing and managing large sums of money.

Subject: Collaboration Opportunity for Financial Transaction

Dear [Recipient],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to introduce myself as Laurent Mpeti Kabila, son of the late President Laurent Desire Kabila of the Democratic Republic of Congo. I am reaching out to you through a recommendation from the Chamber of Commerce, and would like to take this opportunity to establish a professional relationship with your organization.

As you may be aware, my father was a prominent figure in African politics, and his legacy continues to be felt. After his untimely passing in 2001, various assets were left unaccounted for, and it is my responsibility, as mandated by my father's colleagues, to ensure that these assets are utilized for the greater good.

I am writing to propose a collaborative opportunity for the transfer of a substantial sum of money and precious diamonds, valued at $25 million and thousands of carats, respectively. These assets are currently held in a secure vault with a reputable security firm in Europe and South Africa.

The assets in question originated from a defense budget allocated by my father, which was intended for the purchase of arms to secure the country's defense. Unfortunately, due to unforeseen circumstances, these funds were not utilized as intended.

I would like to propose a mutually beneficial arrangement, where your organization would act as the intermediary for the transfer of these assets into your nominated bank account. In recognition of your cooperation and partnership, it has been agreed that you would be entitled to a 5.5% commission upon successful completion of this transaction.

I would appreciate the opportunity to discuss this proposal in more detail and explore the possibilities of working together. Please let me know if you are interested, and we can schedule a call to discuss the next steps.

Thank you for your consideration, and I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

Laurent Mpeti Kabila

subject: Rewritten L1: Funds Investment
body:
Subject: Funds Investment

SENIOR ADVOCATE OF NIGERIA BARR. WILLIAMS FALANA (SAN)

Dear Sir,

I am Barrister Williams Falana, a member of the Nigeria Bar Association (NBA).

Your contact reached me through the World Business Encyclopaedia. Hence, I made up my mind to introduce this business to you in confidence for the mutual benefit of both of us.

The sum of USD 48M (Forty-eight million United States Dollars) was lodged into a security company here in the Country by the late Head of State (General Sani Abacha) for safe-keeping. This money was lodged in security vaults/boxes and labelled as personal belongings, and as such, the security company does not know the true content of the boxes. This money was originally meant to be used for his political campaign.

Because I was his family Attorney, as such, he confided in me with the relevant document papers relating to this deposit before he died of cardiac arrest. As a matter of fact, we have concluded all arrangements with an offshore Security Company to move this money as a consignment through diplomatic means to their offshore affiliated office, where you will be required to put claim to the consignment as the bonafide beneficiary of the consignment.

You should know that this business is safe and 100% risk-free, as it does not involve drug money or Terrorist funds. If you are interested in carrying out this transaction with me, 20% will be for you for your assistance, 5% for general expenses, and 75% for us. You are required to send by e-mail, immediately, your full name and address, which I will use to draft an agreement that will guide and protect both of us in this transaction, also which will be used to effect the change of ownership of the consignment to your name as the beneficiary of the consignment.

Also, send me your telephone and fax numbers for easy communication. Once you notify me of your willingness by sending the above requirements, this transaction will be concluded within 7 (seven) working days. I will be waiting for your urgent reply. You can contact me at:

[Email address]

Please reply to this mail.

subject: Rewritten L2: [ILUG] IMPORTANT.
body:
Subject: Business Proposal and Partnership Opportunity

From: Dr. Samuel Eboka, Chief Accountant/Internal Auditor, Contract Award Committee (CAC), Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC)

Dear esteemed colleague,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to you in a professional capacity as the Chief Accountant/Internal Auditor of the Contract Award Committee (CAC) of the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC). I am reaching out to you with a sensitive and confidential business proposal that requires your expertise and discretion.

As part of our regular auditing and accounting processes, we have identified a surplus amount of $20,500,000 resulting from an over-invoiced bill associated with one of our contracts. In compliance with our Ministry's financial regulations, we are unable to utilize this amount for our own purposes due to a restriction on foreign account ownership for civil servants.

We have carefully considered this surplus and are seeking a reliable partner to assist us in transferring the funds internationally. This would require a trusted individual or entity with a valid bank account in a reputable financial institution. The funds would be transferred via an International Bank Draft, which would be payable in any of the major financial centers around the world.

I understand that this opportunity may be of interest to you, and I would like to extend an invitation for a discussion regarding a potential partnership. This would involve a mutually beneficial agreement, where you would receive a suitable compensation for your assistance. I am confident that we can build a strong working relationship, and I am willing to travel to your country to finalize the arrangements upon confirmation of our mutual interest.

Please note that we have taken all necessary precautions to ensure that this transaction is conducted in a transparent and compliant manner, with the full approval of our Ministry. I would be delighted to provide you with more information and answer any questions you may have.

Thank you for considering this proposal, and I look forward to the possibility of collaborating with you.

Best regards,

Dr. Samuel Eboka
Chief Accountant/Internal Auditor, Contract Award Committee (CAC), NNPC

Subject: Invitation to the 2003 China International Electronic Equipments and Components & Surface Mounting Technology Fair

Dear [Recipient],

We are pleased to invite you to the 11th China International Electronic Equipments and Components & Surface Mounting Technology Fair, which will be held at the China Hi-Tech Fair Exhibition Centre in Shenzhen from April 1st to 4th, 2003.

The Ministry of Science and Technology of People's Republic of China, in conjunction with the Chinese Institute of Electronics (CIE) and other esteemed organizations, has been instrumental in organizing this event. The exhibition is expected to bring together leading electronic component suppliers and manufacturers from around the world, providing a unique opportunity for industry professionals to network, learn, and conduct business.

As a valued member of our industry, we would like to extend an exclusive invitation for you to join us at this premier event. The exhibition will showcase the latest advancements in electronic components, technologies, and manufacturing processes, and we believe that your attendance will be a valuable contribution to the success of this event.

To facilitate your participation, we have arranged a special registration package, which includes:

- Free admission to the exhibition
- Access to exclusive networking sessions and seminars
- Opportunities to establish business relationships with leading industry players
- A comprehensive exhibition guide and directory

To take advantage of this offer, please visit our website at http://www.e-dowell.com and register online. You will be required to provide your name, company, and contact information to receive your confirmation and registration details.

We look forward to welcoming you to the 2003 China International Electronic Equipments and Components & Surface Mounting Technology Fair.

subject: Rewritten L3: Tell Me Where to Send Your Health Card
body:
Subject: Important Update from HR: Employee Benefits and Claims Procedure

From: Jennifer Lee [jlee@ABCcorp.com](mailto:jlee@ABCcorp.com)
To: All ABC Corp Employees
CC: HR Department [hr@ABCcorp.com](mailto:hr@ABCcorp.com)

Dear Team,

As part of our ongoing efforts to ensure seamless employee benefits and claims processing, we are introducing a new online portal to facilitate easy access and management of your health insurance claims. This portal will also provide you with a comprehensive overview of your benefits package and enable you to track the status of your claims in real-time.

To access the new portal, please click on the following link: https://benefits.abcportal.com/login

In order to ensure a smooth transition, we kindly request that all employees update their credentials by verifying their employee ID and password. If you have any issues with the login process, please do not hesitate to contact our IT department at [it@ABCcorp.com](mailto:it@ABCcorp.com) or extension 555.

In addition to the new portal, we are also excited to announce a few enhancements to our benefits package, including:

- Priority access to our employee assistance program (EAP)
- Increased coverage for mental health services
- A new flexible spending account (FSA) program

As always, we appreciate your feedback and suggestions on how we can continue to improve our benefits and claims process. If you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to reach out to the HR department.

Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

Best regards,

Jennifer Lee
HR Representative
ABC Corporation
